stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Volcanoes erupt at a point called a vent and release material both upward and outward.
  • Two of the materials commonly released during volcanic eruptions are tephra and lava.
  • Tephra rises higher into the atmosphere than lava does.
  • Tephra consists of fragments of rock and ash propelled by gas, falling back to the ground after being ejected.
  • Lava flows downhill as a liquid stream of molten rock.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between tephra and lava. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Rising from the vent of a volcano, tephra eventually falls back to the ground as fragments of rock and ash.
Tephra and lava are both released from a volcano's vent, eventually settling on the ground.
Tephra travels higher into the atmosphere than lava does after a volcanic eruption.
Although tephra and lava emerge from the same vent, they move very differently.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between tephra and lava, noting that they are both released from a volcano's vent and eventually settle on the ground.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes how tephra moves; it does not emphasize a similarity between tephra and lava. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a difference between tephra and lava, noting that tephra travels higher than lava; it does not emphasize a similarity. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence acknowledges that tephra and lava emerge from the same vent, it emphasizes that they move very differently, which is a difference rather than a similarity.
